title: Introduction to creating maps with leaflet
description:
We warmly welcome you to our last virtual meetup of the year on "Introduction to creating maps with leaflet" by Dr. Laurie Baker.

📆 November 27th, 2021 3 PM - 5 PM (GMT+3)

Our trainer➡️ Dr. Laurie is a disease ecologist and marine biologist by training. She earned her BSc and MSc in marine biology at the University of St. Andrews and Dalhousie University (MSc) respectively, and her doctorate in epidemiology at the University of Glasgow. Dr. Laurie is a certified RStudio Instructor with a keen interest in programming, data science, and the use of novel data sources in research. The core of her research focuses on spatial and temporal patterns in human and biological systems including disease spread, animal movement, and fisheries management.

We will share the zoom link and event details with the registered participants.

All are welcome!

For any queries or comments, contact us on;

Email address: nairobi@rladies.org

title: Working with Databases in R
description:
In collaboration with #NairobiR, we are delighted to welcome you to our next joint virtual meetup on 12th June as the invited trainer Christopher Maronga takes us through Working with Databases in R!

📆 June 12th, 2021 11 AM- 1 PM (EAT)

To sign up ➡️https://bit.ly/databasesinR

About the Trainer, ➡️ Christopher Maronga is a Data Manager (Clinical) at KEMRI-Wellcome Trust. He has over 6 years of experience in health research data management and analysis mostly working using R, SQL and REDCap. Leveraging R’s API capability to seamlessly extract data from data repositories such as RDBMs and web-based databases such as REDCap has made data use very easy and efficient across industries.

In this talk, Maronga will share with us practically gained experience in accessing these databases, and how you can turn your R into a powerful tool for accessing MySQL database and writing SQL code to pull and query your data from within the R environment.
We will share the zoom link and event details with the registered participants.

All are welcome!

Looking forward to e-seeing y'all.

For any inquiries or comments, contact us on;

Email address: nairobi@rladies.org

LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/in/r-ladies-nairobi-2572841b3/

Twitter: @RLadiesNairobi

title: #rstats hangout with Shannon Pileggi
description:
We are excited to join forces with R-Ladies Cologne and host a data hangout with Shannon Pileggi **✨**

**About the data hangout**
For the first time, we will be meeting on a Twitter
space to have our data hangout, an informal Q&A session where we will talk with Shannon about #rstats, data, career trajectories and all the things we all love 💜

**About Shannon**
Shannon Pileggi PhD is a Data Scientist with more than ten years of experience partnering on data analysis with diverse stakeholders in academic, corporate, tech, public health, and clinical research settings. An enthusiastic educator and statistical collaborator, Shannon started her career in academia as a Lecturer with the Institute for Quantitative Theory and Methods at Emory University, and later as an Assistant Professor at California Polytechnic State University, San Luis Obispo.
When she transitioned to the industry in 2019, Shannon leveraged open source and community resources to adopt modern programming practices and workflows in R. In 2020, Shannon began to give back to the R community through blogging and developing R-Ladies workshops; she currently serves as a member of the R-Ladies Global Team.
Shannon spends her days wrangling data, creating reproducible reports, building shiny apps, developing R packages, and mentoring junior Data Scientists at The Prostate Cancer Clinical Trials Consortium, housed under Memorial Sloan Kettering Cancer Center.
You can find her when piping hot data on her website ([pipinghotdata.com](https://www.pipinghotdata.com/)), GitHub ([@shannonpileggi](https://github.com/shannonpileggi)) or Twitter ([@PipingHotData](https://twitter.com/PipingHotData)).

**Fancy joining us?**
To join, check out our Twitter and make sure to tune in on June 2, 2022, at 7:00 PM GMT + 3/ 6:00 PM GMT + 2/12 PM ET.

title: #30DayChartChallenge: Nicola's Experience
description:
Greetings members!

✨ We welcome you to our virtual meetup:

Topic: **#30DayChartChallenge: Nicola's Experience**

Date: **May 26th, 2022, 8 PM - 10 PM (GMT+3)**

Our speaker: **Dr Nicola Rennie**

**Biography:** Nicola Rennie is a statistician and data scientist, passionate about using R to solve problems. She currently works as a data scientist at Jumping Rivers, having previously completed her PhD in statistics and operational research at Lancaster University.

**Abstract of the talk:** During April 2022, I participated in the #30DayChartChallenge - a data visualisation challenge where participants create a new chart each day based on the daily prompt. In this talk, I'll discuss why and how I created 30 charts, the aspects I found most challenging, and some interesting things I discovered in R along the way!

We will share the zoom link with the registered participants.

***All are welcome!***

For any comments & questions, contact us on;

title: Job hunting for Data Analysts: Why it's hard and what one can do about it
description:
Happy new year 2022, members!

We welcome you to our first meetup of the year:

Topic: Job hunting for Data Analysts: Why it's hard and what one can do about it

Date: February 19th, 2022 11 AM - 1 PM (GMT+3)

Trainer: Oscar Baruffa

Biography: Oscar Baruffa is a Data Specialist helping non-profits achieve the United Nations Sustainable Development goals and is currently working in the international development sector directing the development of a full data and analytics pipeline. Previous roles have seen him modeling energy consumption of buildings, auditing energy use and carbon emission metrics, and closely following macro and micro-economic trends in plastic recycling value chains. His 15-year career has seen him on both sides of the hiring table many times and has valuable insights to share.

➡️ The presentation will aim to demystify the hiring process and give concrete examples of how to improve one's chances.

Oscar will explain why it is that applying to hundreds of jobs can still result in getting no interviews or even responses. He’ll help to alleviate the "sense of failure" from participants. He’ll then go on to the single biggest improvement he thinks people can make, and that's to their CV and cover letter. He’s seen so many poor ones that can be improved in the same way. He’ll also touch on a strategy he uses when job hunting to conserve his energy and emotional state!

We will share the zoom link with the registered participants.

All are welcome!

For any comments & questions, contact us on;

Email address: nairobi@rladies.org

LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/in/r-ladies-nairobi-2572841b3/

Twitter: @RLadiesNairobi

title: 🌍 R-Ladies Global Rotating Curation on Bluesky: Amplifying African Voices in DS
description:
In collaboration with **R-Ladies Gaborone**, we’re excited to host a special meetup exploring the **R-Ladies Global Rotating Curation** initiative and how it’s creating space for African voices in the global R community.

🔊 Talks & Speakers:
⚙️ Simisani Ndaba — Member of the global R-Ladies admin team, will offer a behind-the-scenes look at how the curation program works, why it matters, and how to get involved.
💜 Njoki Njuki — Data Analyst, MSc student, and recent #WeAreRLadies curator, will share her lived experience curating for the global R-Ladies account and reflections on community, code, and representation.

✨ What to expect:

* Tips for engaging in the global R community
* Insights into curation as a tool for community storytelling
* A spotlight on the importance of visibility for African data scientists

🗓️June 26th, 2025
🕒 1 p\.m\. EAT \(12 p\.m\. for SAST \| CAT\)
📍 Online: [https://bit.ly/4k2J9bO](https://bit.ly/4k2J9bO) (participant password: 202020)

Join us for an inspiring and informative session that celebrates leadership, storytelling, and data science across the continent!

#RLadies #WeAreRLadies #DataScience #RStats #AfricaInData
